title: [FIRRTL] FirRegLowering mux-to-if conversion check could be more performant
body:
The logic that determines whether to emit a `comb.mux` or build an `sv.if` for a given register needs to know if the `comb.mux` is reachable from the register. This is currently implemented as a forward dataflow walk from the register, faning out until it reaches another register or an instance.

https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/1705306f41dea8243d72c262e5c30c87c458caf8/lib/Conversion/SeqToSV/FirRegLowering.cpp#L343-L354

This can be a performance concern--the current implementation could take 20% longer in SeqToSV than the previous implementation for real designs.

There are at least a couple ways to improve this.

A simple way is to change the filtering logic in the getForwardSliceSimple callback to have a more restricted allow/deny list. Right now we have a deny list that is totally conservative--we know we can stop when we reach another register or an instance. A better approach might be to use an allow list--we know we want to fan out through muxes, but there are other "wiring" ops like array create/get that should also be allowed. If we can come up with an allowlist that is restrictive but not fragile, that would be an easy way to improve performance.

A more involved way would be to step back and realize that this is a general reachability problem. I don't think there is anything immediately useful upstream in MLIR, but with the right data structure and algorithm, this should be solvable efficiently. Let the nerd sniping begin...
